example: Lí-hó! Góa sī sin-lâng. (Hello! I am new here.)
To-siā lí ê pang-chān. (Thank you for your help.)
Chit pún-mī hó-tsia̍h! (This noodle dish is delicious!)
Ū tsuí khó-í lim--bē? (Is there water to drink?)
Chài-kiàn! Koh-kái-chêng. (Goodbye! See you next time.)
Chá-an! Lí chia̍h-pá bô? (Good morning! Have you eaten?)
Àm-an, khùn-hó! (Good night, sleep well!)
Lí hó-bô? — Hó, to-siā! (How are you? — Fine, thanks!)
Góa chin-hó, to-siā. Lí leh? (I'm fine, thanks. And you?)
Lí chia̍h-pá--bôe? — Chia̍h-pá ah! (Have you eaten? — Yes, I have!)
Āu-pái koh-chài! (See you next time!)
Pháiⁿ-sè, góa bô-ì-sù. (Sorry, I didn't mean it.)
Lí kiò siáⁿ miâ? Góa kiò Ah-míng. (What is your name? My name is Ah-Ming.)
Góa kiò William. (My name is William.)
Hòaⁿ-hí jīn-bat lí! (Nice to meet you!)
To-siā! — M̄-bián kheh-khì! (Thank you! — You're welcome!)
Sī, góa ū. (Yes, I have/do.)
M̄-sī, góa bô. (No, I don't.)
Lí tó-uī lâng? — Góa sī Tâi-uân lâng. (Where are you from? — I'm Taiwanese.)
Góa sī Má-lâi-se-a lâng. (I am Malaysian.)
Pháiⁿ-sè, góa thiaⁿ-bô. (Sorry, I don't understand.)
Góa chai-iáⁿ ah! (I understand now!)
Chhiáⁿ kóng khah bān! Góa thiaⁿ-bô. (Please speak slowly! I don't understand.)
Chhiáⁿ koh kóng chit-pái, hó--bô? (Can you please say that again?)
Pháiⁿ-sè, góa iáu teh o̍h Hô-ló-uē. (Sorry, I'm still learning Hokkien.)
Choán tò-pêng, jiân-āu tit-khì. (Turn left, then go straight.)
Choán chiàⁿ-pêng, jiân-āu tit-khì. (Turn right, then go straight.)
Tit-khì, jiân-āu choán chiàⁿ-pêng. (Go straight, then turn right.)
Tī chia choán tò-pêng. (Turn left here.)
Tī chêng-bīn ū chi̍t-ê chhī-tiûⁿ. (There is a market ahead.)
Chhù tī āu-bīn. (The house is at the back.)
Beh án-choáⁿ khì chhī-tiûⁿ? (How do I get to the market?)
Góa beh chit-pōe tê. (I would like one cup of tea.)
Chit-ê jōa-chōe chîⁿ? (How much does this cost?)
Siuⁿ-kùi ah! Ē-sái khah pêng--bô? (Too expensive! Can you make it cheaper?)
Ē-sái khah pêng--bô? Góa beh bóe nn̄g-ê. (Can it be cheaper? I'll buy two.)
Góa pak-tó͘ iau, beh khì chia̍h-pn̄g. (I'm hungry, I want to go eat.)
Góa chia̍h-pá--a, to-siā! (I'm full, thank you!)
Góa beh sǹg-siàu. (Bill please.)
Ū chúi--bô? (Do you have water?)
Bô-iàu-kín, bô-iàu-kín! (Never mind, never mind!)
Tán chi̍t-ē! Góa beh khì bóe. (Wait a moment! I want to go buy something.)
Kiù-miā! (Help!)
Kiò kéng-chhat lâi! (Call the police!)
Góa beh chhōe i-seng. (I need to find a doctor.)
Sù-khòng-sù. (404 — zero in the middle.)
It-chheng kho͘. (One thousand dollars.)
Kin-á-jit kúi-tiám? (What time is it today?)
Kúi-tiám ah? (What time is it?)
Góa pīⁿ-tio̍h. (I've fallen ill.)
Kin-á-jit chin joa̍h. (Today is very hot.)
Góa chin kôaⁿ. (I'm very cold.)
Góa ê kha tio̍h-siong. (My leg is injured.)
Chia ē-thiàⁿ. (It hurts here.)
Piān-só͘ tī tó-uī? (Where is the toilet?)
Lú-koán tī uī? (Where is the hotel?)
Góa beh it-ê pâng-king. (I want one room.)
Góa beh chē kè-thêng-chhia. (I want to take a taxi.)
Kong-chhia tī tó-uī thêng? (Where does the bus stop?)
Hóe-chhia kúi-tiám khí? (What time does the train leave?)
Chhia-chām tī tó-uī? (Where is the station?)
Poe-ki-tiûⁿ ū loán-bīn? (Does the airport have a lounge?)
Chit-tiuⁿ phiò gōa-chōe? (How much is one ticket?)
Chit-ê biō chin súi. (This temple is very beautiful.)
Lán khì iā-chhī! (Let's go to the night market!)
Góa chia̍h sò͘-si̍t. (I eat vegetarian.)
Chhiáⁿ bē-lah. (Please make it not spicy.)
Lín-má! Kan-poe! (Cheers! Bottoms up!)
Góa bô-lō͘. Ē-sái pang-chān góa bô? (I'm lost. Can you help me?)
Chhiáⁿ hō͘ góa chúi. (Please give me water.)
Io̍h-pâng tī tó-uī? (Where is the pharmacy?)
Chó͘ góa khì i-īⁿ. (Take me to the hospital.)
Chit-goe̍h sī saⁿ-goe̍h. (This month is March.)
